title: Staging and Committing
content:
### Staging Changes with git add

The staging area is where you prepare changes before committing.

```bash
git add file.txt              # Stage a specific file
git add .                     # Stage all changes in current dir
git add -A                    # Stage all changes in repo
git add -p                    # Stage interactively (hunk by hunk)
```

### Committing with git commit

```bash
git commit -m "Add login feature"
git commit -am "Fix typo"     # Stage tracked files + commit
git commit --amend            # Edit last commit message or add changes
```

**Commit message conventions:** Use present tense, imperative mood ("Add feature" not "Added feature").

### Viewing History with git log

```bash
git log                        # Full history
git log --oneline              # One line per commit
git log --graph                # ASCII branch graph
git log --oneline --graph --all  # Complete picture
git log --pretty=format:"%h %s" # Custom format
```

### .gitignore Basics

Create a `.gitignore` file to prevent tracking certain files:

```
# Ignore compiled files
*.o
*.exe

# Ignore environment files
.env
node_modules/
```

Place `.gitignore` in the repo root and commit it.
codeExample:
echo "console.log('hello')" > app.js
git add app.js
git commit -m "Add app.js entry point"
git log --oneline --graph

quiz.question: Which flag stages changes interactively by hunk?
